title: Epigenetic ageing clocks for three threatened Australian fish species
claim_summary: DNA methylation patterns can serve as reliable epigenetic clocks to predict age in three threatened Australian freshwater fish species, supporting conservation management applications.
stated_reason: Coauthor alerted the journal that results in a figure are inaccurate and do not represent a real relationship between age and DNA methylation.
selection_method: Retraction Watch ecology category 2025, individual event. Cited 41 times - low fame, no mainstream press. Niche: conservation epigenetics.
publisher_fetch: blocked (Wiley 403 to automated fetch); verified via Crossref + Retraction Watch + notice DOI
corpus_scope: Epigenetic clock methodology in threatened species conservation biology
